FEE PAID FOR: STIPULATION AND [PROPOSED] ORDER EXTENDING TIME TO RESPOND TO PETITION TO VACATE REAL ESTATE ARBITRATION **FIRST APPEARANCE FEE** (TRANSACTION ID # 76615234) FILED BY RESPONDENT KOKKARI LTD. , A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PARKFRONT INVESTMENTS LLC'S PETITION TO VACATE REAL ESTATE ARBITRATION AWARD. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PARKFRONT'S PETITION IS DENIED. THE ARBITRATOR DID NOT EXCEED THEIR AUTHORITY UNDER CCP SECTION 1286.2(A)(4). IT DOES NOT ARBITRARILY REWRITE OR VIOLATE THE LEASE FOR THE ARBITRATOR TO FIND ONE ESTIMATE TO BE "MORE ACCURATE" THAN ANOTHER FOR MULTIPLE REASONS, ONLY ONE OF WHICH IS RELATED TO AN INTERPRETATION OF THE LEASE. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE ARBITRATOR ALSO DID NOT "ENTIRELY AGREE" WITH THE REASONING UNDERLYING THE ESTIMATE THAT IT ADOPTED; THE ARBITRATOR'S TASK WAS TO PICK THE BETTER OF TWO ESTIMATES. CONVERSELY, KOKKARI'S REQUEST TO CONFIRM THE ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. ALTHOUGH THE COURT HAS TAKEN KOKKARI'S PETITION OFF CALENDAR, KOKKARI'S TIMELY FILED OPPOSITION REQUESTS TO CONFIRM THE ARBITRATION AWARD, WHICH THIS COURT HAS THE AUTHORITY TO GRANT. (SEE C.C.P. SECTION 1285.2). PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: NATALIE WILLS, CSR 14607 (T: 650.773.4778, E: 7FOLDREPORTING@GMAIL.COM)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, KOKKARI LTD.'S PETITION TO CONFIRM CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD IS OFF CALENDAR. INSUFFICIENT NOTICE UNDER CCP 1005(B). JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: NATALIE WILLS, CSR 14607 (T: 650.773.4778, E: 7FOLDREPORTING@GMAIL.COM)

Glenn Zwang via Zoom for Respondent/Moving party Kokkari Ltd.
Not Reported

Hearing is held. Argued re amount. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

RESPONDENT KOKKARI LTD. , A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY's Motion For Attorney'S Fees
is GRANTED. Respondent is the prevailing party entitled to recovery of reasonable attorney's fees under the Lease in the amount of $51,063 for reasonable hours at reasonable rates.
Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)

Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jul-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: KOKKARI LTD.'S PETITION TO CONFIRM CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD

Appearances: Susan Allison and Joel Deutsch of Jeffer Mangels Butler & Mitchell LLP (310.203.8080) for Parkfront Investments LLC; Glenn P. Zwang of Buchalter (415.227.0770) for Kokkari Ltd.

This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice under CCP 1005(b). Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Natalie Wills, CSR 14607 (T: 650.773.4778, E: 7foldreporting@gmail.com) (501/GLW)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Vacate Real Estate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jul-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PARKFRONT INVESTMENTS LLC'S PETITION TO VACATE REAL ESTATE ARBITRATION AWARD

Appearances: Susan Allison and Joel Deutsch of Jeffer Mangels Butler & Mitchell LLP (310.203.8080) for Parkfront Investments LLC; Glenn P. Zwang of Buchalter (415.227.0770) for Kokkari Ltd.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Parkfront's Petition is DENIED. The Arbitrator did not exceed their authority under CCP Section 1286.2(a)(4). It does not arbitrarily rewrite or violate the lease for the Arbitrator to find one estimate to be "more accurate" than another for multiple reasons, only one of which is related to an interpretation of the lease. The Court notes that the Arbitrator also did not "entirely agree" with the reasoning underlying the estimate that it adopted; the Arbitrator's task was to pick the better of two estimates.

Conversely, Kokkari's request to confirm the arbitration award is GRANTED. Although the Court has taken Kokkari's Petition off calendar, Kokkari's timely filed Opposition requests to confirm the arbitration award, which this Court has the authority to grant. (See C.C.P. Section 1285.2). Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Natalie Wills, CSR 14607 (T: 650.773.4778, E: 7foldreporting@gmail.com) (501/GLW)
